description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a spiny lobster, featuring a robust, segmented exoskeleton with a reddish-brown coloration and lighter spots along its body. The cephalothorax is covered in spines and tubercles, providing a rough, armored appearance, while the abdomen is elongated and ends in a broad, fan-shaped tail. Two long, whip-like antennae extend from the head, curving outward and backward, and several smaller antennules are also visible. The lobster has five pairs of walking legs, each jointed and spiny, but lacks prominent claws, distinguishing it from true lobsters. The eyes are small, dark, and set on short stalks at the front of the head. The overall texture is hard and rough, with a glossy sheen on the carapace and limbs.
description: A spiny lobster with a segmented, spiny exoskeleton, long antennae, and no large claws, colored reddish-brown with lighter spots.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated body, segmented tail, and long antennae extending backward.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the broad, spiny carapace, symmetrical walking legs, and fan-shaped tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again displaying the segmented abdomen, spiny legs, and extended antennae.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the head, small eyes on short stalks, and the prominent, forward-projecting antennae.
